cam-b1-dist-01.narrative: Re-area a campus uplink and re-group the HSRP gateway in one change — two independent stories: a routing (OSPF) impact and a first-hop-redundancy (HSRP) impact.
cam-b1-dist-01.snippet:
interface GigabitEthernet1
 ip ospf 1 area 1
interface Vlan100
 no standby 1 ip 10.40.1.254
 standby 2 ip 10.40.1.254

cam-core-01.narrative: Move a core↔distribution link into a new OSPF area and drain a service-core BGP uplink at once — an OSPF story and a BGP story in a single change.
cam-core-01.snippet:
interface GigabitEthernet3
 ip ospf 1 area 2
router bgp 65400
 neighbor 10.0.0.169 shutdown

dc-spine-02.narrative: Fat-finger the eBGP peer AS on a fabric uplink — the OPEN is rejected (bad peer AS) and the leaf session never establishes.

branch-rtr-01.narrative: Prepend the AS-path on the branch's advertised LAN (a routine traffic-steering edit). Two ASes away, across the WAN transit, a campus-core inbound filter keys on the over-prepended path and drops 10.50.1.0/24 outright — so the campus loses reachability to the branch LAN. A policy change at the source cascades into a router whose own config never changed; you cannot read cam-core in isolation.
branch-rtr-01.snippet:
route-map TO-PEER permit 10
 match ip address prefix-list RFC1918
 set as-path prepend 65501 65501

dc-bleaf-01.narrative: Fat-finger the peer-AS on both of the border-leaf's uplinks to the Internet edge — the eBGP sessions never re-establish, and the external prefixes the edge injects are blackholed across the data center (traffic to them is silently dropped).
dc-bleaf-01.snippet:
router bgp 65011
 neighbor 10.0.0.114 remote-as 65299
 neighbor 10.0.0.118 remote-as 65299

branch-rtr-02.narrative: Re-range the local MPLS label space on the branch WAN router — local label bindings are re-allocated and downstream LSP label mappings update; LDP router-id reachability is also flagged.
branch-rtr-02.snippet:
mpls ldp router-id Loopback0 force
mpls label range 100 199
mpls ldp graceful-restart
mpls ldp session protection

dc-leaf-01.narrative: Move the VXLAN VTEP onto a different source loopback — every VXLAN tunnel re-establishes with the new source IP; overlay segments flap during the transition.
dc-leaf-01.snippet:
interface nve1
  source-interface loopback9
  member vni 10010
  member vni 10020
  member vni 50001 associate-vrf

dc-leaf-02.narrative: Drop the vPC peer-keepalive on a leaf — the keepalive heartbeat is lost and, if the peer-link also fails, both switches assume primary role (split-brain).
dc-leaf-02.snippet:
vpc domain 10
  role priority 200
  delay restore 150
  auto-recovery
interface port-channel10
  vpc peer-link

cam-core-02.narrative: Add a PIM RP that exists on no interface and is not IGP-reachable — groups mapped to it fail to build their shared tree and multicast forwarding stops.
cam-core-02.snippet:
ip multicast-routing
ip pim rp-address 10.255.8.1
ip pim rp-address 192.0.2.99

dc-fw-01.narrative: Remove aaa new-model on the firewall — the entire AAA framework is disabled and management auth falls back to line passwords or none (lockout / open-access risk).

wan-core-01.narrative: Remove the only NTP server on a WAN core router — the device loses time sync; clock drift breaks logging correlation, certificate validation, and time-based ACLs.

wan-core-02.narrative: Remove the SNMP community on a WAN core router — NMS polling and trap delivery stop; the device goes dark to monitoring.

svc-core-01.narrative: Remove the syslog destination on a service-core router — security and operational events are no longer exported off-box.

svc-core-02.narrative: Reword the login banner on a service-core router — a benign cosmetic change; the tool correctly rates it informational, not service-affecting.

cam-b1-acc-04.narrative: Remove the DNS name-server on a campus access switch — on-box name resolution fails; impact is contained to the management plane.

cam-b1-acc-02.narrative: Add telnet to the VTY transport on a campus access switch — a security regression that exposes cleartext management access.
cam-b1-acc-02.snippet:
line vty 0 4
 transport input telnet ssh

cam-b1-acc-01.narrative: Change the spanning-tree mode (rapid-pvst → mst) on an access switch — a fabric-wide STP recompute; mismatched modes with neighbors risk loops or blocked ports.

cam-b2-dist-01.narrative: Remove the DHCP relay (helper-address) on a distribution SVI — hosts on that VLAN can no longer reach the DHCP server and fail to obtain addresses.

dc-leaf-03.narrative: Remove BFD from a leaf↔spine uplink — failure detection falls back to slow protocol timers; convergence on a link fault is seconds instead of milliseconds.
dc-leaf-03.snippet:
interface Ethernet1/1
  no bfd interval 250 min_rx 250 multiplier 3
